preamble: The shift from the voiced alveolar affricate [dz] to the voiced postalveolar fricative [ʒ] mirrors the voiceless shift, involving both palatalization and spirantization of the voiced sibilant series.

examples.note: Developments in the voiced affricate series where [dz] simplifies into the corresponding postalveolar fricative.
Stages in the weakening of voiced palatalized stops/affricates toward voiced fricatives.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
